3. Path B run: each fixture dispatched via Agent tool with bead's auto-prompt verbatim; outcomes captured.
4. Path C run: each fixture dispatched via Agent tool with operator hand-curated prompt; outcomes captured.
5. Per-fixture, per-path measurements table with the 7 named metrics.
6. Aggregate stats + failure-mode distribution.
7. Hypothesis verdict section: H1 / H2 / both / inconclusive.
8. Recommendations section: which path for which bead-shape.
9. Report committed at .ddx/executions/<run-id>/ddx-vs-subagent-comparison-2026-05-03.md.
10. Conventional commit ending [<this-bead-id>].
11. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

DEPS (gate execution; bead stays open until these close): refactor children C4-C13 (ddx-358f2457, ddx-c8f79963, ddx-b669bb9f, ddx-06eb05d8, ddx-848069a3, ddx-9228a484, ddx-0b8780db, ddx-c670ef0a, ddx-387a0178).
description:
Hand-curated sub-agent prompts have been outperforming ddx work / ddx try in this session. Two competing hypotheses for why:
H1: ddx execution machinery (the 9 fail-prone layers) is unreliable — failure is in the workers/server code.
H2: Bead authoring quality is insufficient — beads punt investigation; hand-curation papers over the gap.

Both are plausible. Determining the relative contribution requires a 3-path study, NOT the original 2-path proposal.

THREE PATHS

Path A: ddx try / ddx work with the bead's auto-prompt verbatim (current production path).
Path B: Sub-agent dispatched with the bead's auto-prompt verbatim (NOT hand-curated). Same prompt as A; different executor. Isolates ddx machinery from prompt quality.
Path C: Sub-agent dispatched with operator hand-curated prompt (file:line cites, named tests, explicit non-scope, etc.). Establishes the ceiling.

INTERPRETATION
- A vs B differ → ddx machinery is the failure (file follow-ups against the 9 layers).
- B vs C differ → bead authoring quality is the failure (already covered by bead-quality-audit + retrofit).
- A == B << C → both are real, with bead quality being the dominant gap.

METHOD
Pick 5 representative CLOSED beads as fixtures: one trivial fix, one refactor child, one test addition, one multi-file feature, one documentation update. For each fixture, run all 3 paths on a branch from the bead's base-rev (parent of the original work commit). Throwaway commits — the original work is already in main. We're measuring path properties, not producing mergeable code.

MEASUREMENTS
Per path per bead:
- Time-to-first-claim (A) / time-to-first-output (B,C)
- Time-to-merge-eligible-commit (whether or not we merge)
- Attempts (A) / iterations (B,C)
- Status outcomes: success / no_changes / preserved / blocked / errored
- Cost: tokens, USD
- Manual interventions
- Failure mode classified per the 9-layer machinery list

OUTPUT
.ddx/executions/<run-id>/ddx-vs-subagent-comparison-2026-05-03.md with:
- Per-bead per-path table
- Aggregate stats (median + p99)
- Failure-mode distribution
- Hypothesis verdict (H1 vs H2 vs both)
- Recommendations for which path is appropriate for which bead-shape

TIMING
This study runs AFTER the executeloop refactor (C0-C13) lands so the ddx machinery is its post-refactor shape. Running pre-refactor measures behavior we're about to change. RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES + bead-quality-audit + retrofit + refactor must all complete first. This bead is filed now and EXECUTION DEFERRED.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Fixing whatever the study reveals (separate bug beads filed from the study's findings)
- Running against pre-refactor ddx machinery (data we won't act on)
- Comparing different sub-agent harnesses (different concern)

EVIDENCE
- ddx-f339c399 documents the 2026-05-04 ddx try ddx-29058e2a --harness=codex no_changes outcome and is partial Path B-vs-C data favoring the bead-quality hypothesis.

acceptance:
1. TestExecuteBead_IndexLockNotHeldAcrossSubprocess asserts that during an execute-bead run with a mocked long-running harness subprocess, .git/index.lock does not exist (or its mtime is older than the subprocess start) for the duration of the subprocess wait.
2. TestExecuteBead_TrackerLockNotHeldAcrossSubprocess asserts the same property for .ddx/.git-tracker.lock.
3. TestExecuteBead_LocksReacquiredForMutation asserts the locks ARE acquired (briefly) when the post-subprocess git stage/commit and tracker update execute.
4. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/bead/... green.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
ddx work --watch holds .git/index.lock and .ddx/.git-tracker.lock for the entire execute-bead lifetime, including the multi-minute LLM subprocess wait. Observed 2026-05-17: PID 90753 held .git/index.lock for 6+ minutes while a claude subprocess ran, blocking concurrent bead create/update/close operations with 'tracker lock timeout (max elapsed)' errors.

ROOT CAUSE
The execute-bead path in cli/cmd/work.go and the tracker mutation path in cli/internal/bead/ acquire the git index lock / .git-tracker.lock once at the start of a worker iteration and release only at the end, spanning the subprocess.Wait() on the agent harness. Search for lock acquisition sites in cli/cmd/work.go (worker loop), cli/cmd/run.go (run harness invocation), and cli/internal/bead/store.go (tracker write path) — the os.Open/flock or LockFile call wrapping the harness invocation is the offender. Executing agent must grep for index.lock / git-tracker.lock acquisition and confirm exact file:line.

PROPOSED FIX
Refactor so each lock is acquired immediately before its corresponding git/tracker mutation and released immediately after. The LLM subprocess invocation (claude/codex/gemini exec.Cmd.Run or .Wait) must run with no DDx-held mutation lock. Stage-and-commit operations should batch mutations to minimize re-acquire churn.

NON-SCOPE
- Metric emission (separate child)
- Hold-time cap enforcement (separate child)
- Multi-worker concurrency test (separate child)
- Documentation (separate child)
- Changes to lock implementation itself (flock vs file-based); only acquisition scope is in scope here.

CROSS-REF
Parent observation from Fizeau alignment-review 2026-05-17 (AR-2026-05-17-repo).
title: scope git index.lock and tracker.lock to mutation window, not LLM subprocess lifetime

description:
PROBLEM
A live Niflheim worker audit on 2026-06-06 showed that `ddx work` is not yet a stable release-grade autonomous worker. Strong-model escalation exists, but it is not reliably tied to the class of failure. The worker spent repeated attempts on structural or system failures that stronger models cannot fix, while automatic review evidence was empty on attempts that were recorded as successful. This violates the DDx engineering principle "Cheapest Viable, Escalate on Evidence" (docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:39-45): review, runtime, or retry evidence should determine escalation, and only lower-power inability to meet acceptance criteria should push to stronger routes. It also threatens FEAT-010 task-execution reliability because beads can be retried, escalated, or closed without a typed, auditable reason.

This bead is a Niflheim incident/regression closure bead. It is not a duplicate of the narrower queue items listed under INTERSECTIONS; instead it captures the external evidence DDx workers will not otherwise have, and requires a DDx-side regression suite proving those observed scenarios classify correctly before rerun, escalation, operator attention, or close.

BACKGROUND AND OBSERVED NIFLHEIM EVIDENCE
The evidence was gathered from Niflheim worker logs and result bundles, but those files are not load-bearing for this bead. The relevant facts are copied here because an isolated DDx worker cannot access the Niflheim repository or `/tmp` logs.

1. Decomposed parent kept consuming attempts and escalated to a stronger model anyway.
- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-64217cc5`, title "Instrument cold replicator with structured per-operation S3 tracing".
- Observed result bundle: `.ddx/executions/20260606T152006-a4ca7254/result.json` plus worker logs from 2026-06-05/2026-06-06.
- Routing showed escalation exists: repeated routes started at `gpt-5.4-mini` with requested min powers 5, 7, 8, and 9, then reached `gpt-5.5` at requested min power 10.
- Metrics on the bead showed `attempt_count=24`, `total_tokens=19371353`, and average duration about `244112ms`.
- Despite triage recommending `close_decomposed_or_mark_execution_ineligible`, retries continued until the latest event recorded:
  `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale`
  `powerClass=standard`
  `rationale: status: open`
  `orchestrator_action: decompose`
  `reason: parent bead exceeds the Step 0 size threshold and is already decomposed into focused child beads for tracing, counters, tests, and runbook work.`
  `child_beads: niflheim-f03c61a2, niflheim-df407aa8, niflheim-7a421ede, niflheim-0bbed6c9`
  `outcome_reason=decomposed`
- Expected DDx behavior: once the worker has reliable evidence that the bead is a decomposed parent or execution-ineligible parent, no further implementation attempt or model escalation should occur. It should mark execution-ineligible/proposed/operator-attention according to policy and move on.

2. Land/evidence coordination failures were treated like implementation failure and retried expensively.
- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-c75aba30`, title "Materializer per-merge memory footprint OOMs even at concurrent_limit=8".
- Two expensive attempts both used `gpt-5.4-mini`; no useful stronger-model escalation was observed.
- Metrics showed `attempt_count=2`, `total_tokens=65513510`, and average duration about `3306461.5ms`.
- First attempt result: `.ddx/executions/20260606T113550-7b29ee42/result.json`.
  - `status=execution_failed`, `outcome=error`.
  - Implementation result commit was `7aaeb0712...`; a merge commit `2c6f4d6e6` was later observed as an ancestor of HEAD, but the result still recorded a land coordination failure.
  - Error detail:
    `land coordination failed: land advancer: restoring refs/heads/main to ba2d3059f14e834dcb147f2a6db3b294d83b0fac after evidence commit failed: git update-ref refs/heads/main: fatal: update_ref failed for ref 'refs/heads/main': cannot lock ref 'refs/heads/main': is at 5a176b332b182f2a959614f63769d835c47ffd97 but expected 2c6f4d6e6961b8a93742cde66bceec056934655a`
  - `cycle_trace[0].final_decision="success"` and `cycle_trace[0].review_result={}`.
- Second attempt result: `.ddx/executions/20260606T142223-94f8f0f8/result.json`.
  - `status=execution_failed`, `outcome=error`.
  - Error detail:
    `land coordination failed: land advancer: landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s: A .ddx/executions/20260606T150832-af40450b/result.json`
  - `cycle_trace[0].final_decision="success"` and `cycle_trace[0].review_result={}`.
- At observation time the worker had claimed it again (`20260606T152024-a21d8c54`) and edited six files in a fresh isolated worktree:
  `crates/niflheim-core/src/config/mod.rs`, `crates/niflheim-core/src/config/tests.rs`, `crates/niflheim-core/src/config/types.rs`, `crates/niflheim-delta-writer/src/materializer/config.rs`, `crates/niflheim-delta-writer/src/materializer/executor.rs`, and `crates/niflheim-delta-writer/src/materializer/tests.rs`.
- Expected DDx behavior: land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures are system or coordination failures, not model-quality failures. The retry policy should reconcile a successfully landed candidate when possible, retry only the land/evidence step when safe, or park/operator-attention the bead when the landing worktree is dirty. It should not blindly rerun implementation at the same or stronger route.

3. Pre-claim intake/system dirt caused repeated warning churn instead of a clear operator lane.
- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-a62d31ce`, title "Publish competitive benchmark artifacts and provenance metadata".
- A live worker repeatedly ran readiness/intake. The log showed readiness timeout after five minutes, then:
  `preserved pre-dispatch implementation changes in /home/erik/Projects/niflheim under refs/ddx/pre-dispatch/20260606T153517-f7a32618; released niflheim-a62d31ce and continuing watch. dirty paths: .ddx/harness-sessions/-2929681.json. recover with git stash apply refs/ddx/pre-dispatch/20260606T153517-f7a32618`
- Bead events included `pre_claim_intake.warn` with timeout/system-unready reason and generic suggested action.
- Expected DDx behavior: repeated same/systemic pre-claim warnings and dirty generated DDx artifacts should become an operator-visible/system-unready state after the configured threshold, not an indefinite poll/release loop.

4. Automatic reviews were not forcing meaningful quality improvement.
- Recent Niflheim "success" attempts had empty review results:
  - `niflheim-9dea95da`, result `.ddx/executions/20260606T043655-845c77ee/result.json`: closed success, `cycle_trace.review_result={}`, `final_decision="success"`, model `gpt-5.4-mini`, result commit `78e51a168...`.
  - `niflheim-da4e1311`, result `.ddx/executions/20260606T085358-d80ada3e/result.json`: closed success, `cycle_trace.review_result={}`, `final_decision="success"`, model `gpt-5.4-mini`, result commit `d43be3a3...`.
  - `niflheim-c75aba30` attempts listed above: `cycle_trace.final_decision="success"` with `review_result={}` despite overall land failures.
- Manual Claude review earlier improved bead wording and completeness, but the automatic worker path did not show enforceable per-AC review evidence.
- Expected DDx behavior: unless review is explicitly skipped with a durable reason (`runtime.NoReview` or a valid `review:skip` + reason policy), an empty review result must be a review error or operator-attention signal. It must not be treated as approval, success, or evidence of quality improvement.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` contains the normative routing table: only `OutcomeGenuineFailure` can escalate; dirty worktree, transport, quota, auth, setup, timeout, and merge-conflict do not escalate. The live Niflheim behavior shows execute-bead/work outcomes are not consistently reduced to this typed table before retry/escalation decisions.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:305-309` wraps `LandingAdvancer` errors as generic `land advancer` errors. The caller can record an overall `execution_failed` without distinguishing landed-candidate reconciliation, ref/index contention, staged evidence dirt, or dirty landing worktree from implementation failure.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1967-2072` emits pre-claim timeout/system-unready outcomes as warn-only by default and continues implementation when strict intake blocking is off. This permits repeated same-fingerprint/system warnings to look like a live worker instead of a system-unready/operator lane.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2175-2315` handles `PreClaimIntakeTooLargeDecomposed`, but missing/empty decomposition paths can warn and continue. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3826-3831` can recognize execution-ineligible/decomposed state only after the bead has the right metadata. The Niflheim parent evidence shows structural/decomposed-parent outcomes can still reach repeated implementation attempts and power escalation.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3042-3138` treats success disposition/status as closeable once candidate-cycle review/landing has allowed success, and the old post-merge review is optional. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_test.go:799-823` explicitly proves a worker with `Reviewer: nil` can skip review and close the bead. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:168-193` defines `ReviewResult` with all fields optional except the zero-value enum field; no invariant rejects `{}` as a success-path review artifact.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add compact Niflheim-style regression fixtures under `cli/internal/agent/testdata/niflheim_worker_evidence/` or equivalent. The fixtures should encode the four scenario families above: empty review success, land coordination/ref-lock/staged-evidence failure, decomposed-parent no-changes rationale with child beads, and repeated pre-claim system-unready warning. Do not depend on Niflheim checkout paths.
- Introduce or tighten a single execute-bead retry classification boundary that maps attempt result, land result, review result, no-changes rationale, and pre-claim intake outcome into exactly one action: close, retry same tier, escalate min power/profile, reconcile land/evidence, execution-ineligible/proposed, or operator-attention. Wire this boundary into `ddx work`/`ddx try` so `failclass.Classify` is not merely a standalone helper.
- Enforce review evidence on success. For non-skipped success paths, `cycle_trace.review_result` or the equivalent result field must contain a non-empty verdict, per-AC evidence or documented reason, reviewer routing/session metadata when available, and a classification. `{}` is invalid and must prevent close or produce a review-error/operator-attention event. Explicit review skips remain allowed only when they are durable, reasoned, and visible in result JSON.
- Classify land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures separately from implementation/model failures. If the candidate commit or merge is already landed or is an ancestor of the target, reconcile and close/continue without rerunning implementation. If the landing worktree has staged generated evidence or DDx metadata, retry/preserve the land/evidence step or move to operator attention according to the existing local reliability policy; do not escalate the model for this class.
- Classify decomposed-parent/no-changes rationale as execution-ineligible or operator work before another implementation attempt. Stronger models cannot fix parent beads that are already split into executable children.
- Make worker result JSON and event bodies carry enough audit fields to explain the decision: `failure_class`, `retry_action`, `escalation_count`, requested/actual power/profile/model, review status/verdict, and land/reconcile status.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau route selection or hard-code concrete provider/model names. DDx should continue to pass power/profile intent and let Fizeau choose concrete routes.
- Do not edit Niflheim beads or Niflheim implementation code.
- Do not duplicate the low-level index-contention fix tracked by `ddx-2965925e`; reuse or align with it.
- Do not duplicate the post-commit review/land timeout work tracked by `ddx-b2846610`; this bead requires typed classification and review evidence, not another timeout implementation.
- Do not duplicate the repeated pre-claim warn operator-attention bead `ddx-d67f124e`; this bead uses the Niflheim observation as cross-scenario regression coverage.
- Do not implement a full operator-lane product workflow beyond the state transitions/events needed to keep the worker from blind retry or false close.

INTERSECTIONS
- Parent: `ddx-3106674f` (multi-worker hardening and local reliability gates). This bead is the external Niflheim regression/incident closure layer under that reliability epic.
- Dependency: `ddx-2965925e` covers tracker commit index contention. This bead must not reimplement that fix; it must prove land/ref/index/evidence failures classify separately from model failure once the low-level path is hardened.
- Dependency: `ddx-b2846610` covers bounded post-commit review/land. This bead must still require typed review/land outcomes after the timeout wedge is fixed.
- Dependency: `ddx-d67f124e` covers same-fingerprint pre-claim warning escalation. This bead adds the Niflheim `niflheim-a62d31ce` family as regression evidence.
- Related but not a dependency: `ddx-e1a576a7` is an execution-ineligible decomposed parent and a local precedent for the `niflheim-64217cc5` class.
- Related quality framework: `ddx-a9d130d0` and `ddx-ac10e7af` own broader bead lifecycle/operator-lane policy; this bead is concrete worker behavior and evidence gating.

acceptance:
1. `TestChaos_PostLandCommandDoesNotHoldMainGitLock` blocks a post-land command and proves `withMainGitLock` can be acquired concurrently while the command is blocked.
2. Existing post-land failure preservation tests still pass, including `TestLand_PostLandCommandFailurePreserves` or the current equivalent in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go`.
3. Fast-forward and merge landing tests still pass after the refactor.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestChaos_PostLandCommandDoesNotHoldMainGitLock|TestLand_.*PostLand|TestLand_.*FastForward|TestLand_.*Merge'` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
A post-land command can run while `.ddx/.git-tracker.lock` is held, blocking every other worker/operator mutation for up to the post-land command timeout. This violates the TP-021 lock-lifetime contract: arbitrary commands must not execute while the main-git lock is held.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1208-1214 wraps all of `landLocked` in `withMainGitLock`.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1291 and :1369 call `preserveIfPostLandGateFails` inside that locked flow.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1445-1457 runs `PostLandCommand` with a 30 minute timeout.

PROPOSED FIX
- Refactor landing so post-land commands run after the main-git mutation lock is released, while preserving existing land/preserve semantics and evidence rewriting.
- If a post-land command fails, reacquire the lock only for the short preserve/ref/index mutations needed to record that failure.
- Add a channel-blocking post-land test proving another goroutine can acquire the main-git lock while the command is blocked.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not remove post-land command support.
- Do not change the 30 minute post-land command timeout unless a separate spec updates it.
title: agent: run post-land commands outside the main-git lock

description:
PROBLEM (Principle 5 of epic ddx-602ae31a)
Plugin content can live in two tiers — per-project (ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>) and machine-wide global (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>). Runtime resolution must deterministically prefer the project copy when present, fall back to the global copy, and finally to the baked-in default (ddx plugin only). Without a single authoritative resolver, install/update/init/doctor each reinvent path logic and drift.

ROOT CAUSE
- internal/registry/resolve.go:29 `ResolvePlugin(ctx, projectRoot, name)` is the precedence resolver: project path computed at resolve.go:30, global at resolve.go:35, baked-in fallback at resolve.go:44. It must be the only place precedence is decided and must be covered by named tests so install/init/doctor can rely on it.
- internal/registry/resolve.go:15 `GlobalPluginDir(name)` returns ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name> via ddxroot.GlobalDir(); the project path routes through ddxroot.Path() (in-tree vs convention mode handled there).

PROPOSED FIX
1. Ensure `ResolvePlugin` returns (path, layer, err) with layer ∈ {project, global, baked-in} and the exact precedence project → global → baked-in.
2. Add table-driven tests in internal/registry/resolve_test.go pinning both the prefers-project and falls-back-to-global behaviors against a temp HOME/XDG fixture.
3. Do not alter install/update/init/doctor call sites here beyond confirming they call `ResolvePlugin` (those are covered by sibling children).

NON-SCOPE
- Removing the per-project install tier (both tiers coexist).
- Migrating ~/.config/ddx/ legacy paths.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.
- install/update/init/doctor wiring (sibling children).

DEPS
ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — ResolvePlugin routes through both ddxroot.Path() and ddxroot.GlobalDir(); both must be agreed-on before this lands.

GOVERNING ARTIFACTS
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-027-skill-install-topology.md (precedence decision record).
- CLAUDE.md "Persona System" three-layer precedence note.
title: registry: runtime plugin lookup precedence (project → global → baked-in)

2. TestInitProject_DoesNotInstallPlugins (cmd/init_test.go) asserts `ddx init` (no flag) creates <project>/.ddx/ and writes nothing under any plugins/ directory (project or global), so the plugin lazy-resolves.

4. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... green.

description:
PROBLEM (Principle 3 of epic ddx-602ae31a)
`ddx init --global` is the umbrella first-time machine setup: it installs the bundled default ddx plugin globally, links the skill into the agent-tier paths so every harness on the machine discovers it, and writes ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults. Conversely, `ddx init` (no flag) must create <project>/.ddx/ WITHOUT installing any plugin — plugins lazy-resolve, preferring the project copy over global.

ROOT CAUSE
- cmd/init.go:74 routes `--global` to `initGlobal()` (init.go:318), which materializes the default plugin into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx (init.go:319-328), links the skill (init.go:331), and writes global config (init.go:351-353, writeGlobalConfig at init.go:362).
- The no-flag init path must not write into any plugins/ tree; lazy resolution is delegated to ResolvePlugin (internal/registry/resolve.go:29).

PROPOSED FIX
1. Confirm `ddx init --global` produces ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx pointing into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/, plus ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml.
2. Confirm `ddx init` (no flag) creates <project>/.ddx/ but writes nothing under any plugins/ directory.
3. Cover both with named tests against a temp HOME/XDG + temp project.

NON-SCOPE
- Removing the per-project install tier (both tiers coexist).
- Migrating ~/.config/ddx/ legacy paths.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.
- install/update flag wiring and runtime precedence internals (sibling children).

DEPS
ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — init routes through ddxroot.GlobalDir() and ddxroot.Path(); both must be agreed-on before this lands.
Soft-depends on the ResolvePlugin resolver child for lazy-resolution assertions.

GOVERNING ARTIFACTS
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-027-skill-install-topology.md.
title: init: --global one-time machine setup creates agent-tier links; plain init does not install plugins

2. Existing execute_bead_loop_dirty_root_test.go and consecutive_wedge_guard_test.go tests pass.

description:
PROBLEM: When the main checkout has uncommitted tracked changes, the worker exits operator-attention IMMEDIATELY (attempts:0) and an external supervisor relaunches it, which re-checks, finds the same dirty tree, and instant-exits again — observed ~12x tight loop (resource waste + monitor flood) during the source-leak wedge.

ROOT CAUSE: the dirty-root guard at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1531-1554 returns immediately with OperatorAttentionStop{Reason:"dirty_project_root"} and setExit("OperatorAttention",...) with NO cooldown or repeat counter. The existing consecutive_wedge_guard (consecutive_wedge_guard_test.go) is not applied to this path.

PROPOSED FIX: Track consecutive dirty_project_root operator-attention exits with the identical dirty path-set; after N (small default, e.g. 3) emit an escalated stop reason and impose a backoff/cooldown (mirroring work-retry-after) so a supervisor relaunch does not tight-spin. Do NOT change the guard's correctness (still halt on a genuinely dirty root); only break the instant-exit relaunch loop. (Source-leak prevention is L1; this is defense-in-depth on the loop side.)

NON-SCOPE: source-leak prevention (L1); the binary-asset build-leak / clean-tree tolerance (ddx-b320e794 — kept separate).
title: agent: escalate (cooldown/backoff) repeated dirty-project-root operator-attention exits instead of instant-exit spin

description:
PROBLEM
  The conflict-recovery state machine (auto-merge + focused-resolve + close-or-park) is partially extracted but the description cites only the pre-extraction loop lines, leaving the executor without a clear picture of the current API boundary.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_conflict_recovery.go:118 defines RunConflictRecovery(ctx, ConflictRecoveryInput) ConflictRecoveryOutput — the C4 extraction already landed. The bead description still refers to the old inline branch. The caller site is cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:847-878 (loop branches on ShouldAttemptConflictRecovery, then calls RunConflictRecovery). The try.Attempt API (cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:67, func Attempt(ctx, store, beadID, AttemptOpts) (Outcome, error)) does not yet call RunConflictRecovery; conflict recovery remains loop-owned.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Migrate RunConflictRecovery invocation from execute_bead_loop.go:847-878 into try.Attempt (cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go) so Attempt's return Outcome already encodes conflict-recovery disposition.
  - Add try/conflict_recovery.go: thin adapter from ConflictRecoveryOutput to Outcome (Disposition=Merged → OutcomeSuccess, Park/NeedsHuman → OutcomePark).
  - Loop caller (execute_bead_loop.go:847) delegates to try.Attempt instead of calling RunConflictRecovery directly; removes the inline branch.
  - Add TestAttempt_ConflictRecovered_ReturnsMergedOutcome and TestAttempt_ConflictUnresolvable_ReturnsPark to cli/internal/agent/try/attempt_test.go.

NON-SCOPE
  - Deleting the attempted/hookFailed maps (C7, ddx-06eb05d8).
  - Heartbeat extraction (C11, ddx-0b8780db).
  - File renames (C13, ddx-387a0178).

INTERSECTIONS
- P1: fail-open at the layer boundary when conflict recovery is not applicable or cannot complete cleanly.
- P2: conflict recovery owns only its own conditions; the loop should delegate rather than infer recovery policy.
- P3: the worker panel should surface conflict-recovery transitions and any degraded path.
- P4: a bad bead X must not spill into bead Y; conflict recovery stays per-bead.

1. Add `TestDDxRoot_BareInTreeFallsThroughToXDG` in `cli/internal/ddxroot` proving that a project root containing `.ddx/` without `.ddx/config.yaml` falls through to the XDG project path instead of returning the bare in-tree directory.

description:
PROBLEM: `ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot)` currently treats any `<projectRoot>/.ddx/` directory as an initialized DDx project state root. When `projectRoot=$HOME`, the user-global `~/.ddx/` directory can shadow the intended XDG project state root and bifurcate state.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE: `cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:Path()` uses `os.Stat(InTree(projectRoot))` plus `info.IsDir()` as the only in-tree check. `ExistingPath` uses the same bare-directory contract. The actual initialization marker is `.ddx/config.yaml`, written by `ddx init` at `cli/cmd/init.go:236`.

PROPOSED FIX: Replace the bare directory check in `Path` and `ExistingPath` with an initialized in-tree check that requires `<projectRoot>/.ddx/config.yaml`. Add a small helper such as `isInitializedInTree(dir string) bool` and keep fallback behavior unchanged: if no initialized in-tree root exists, use `filepath.Join(projectsRoot(), projectIdentity(ctx, projectRoot))` and preserve existing bootstrap behavior.

NON-SCOPE: Do not rename `~/.ddx/` to `~/.config/ddx/`. Do not address multi-project federation collisions. Do not change TLS-renew scheduling or rotation logic. Do not update the large cross-package test fixture set in this bead except where needed for `internal/ddxroot` tests. Do not add orphan home-state startup warnings in this bead.
title: ddxroot: require config.yaml before using in-tree state root

4. lefthook run pre-commit passes (covers markdownlint/spec validation on the three edited files).
description:
PROBLEM
FEAT-008, FEAT-010, and FEAT-019 specs lack the documentation sections that make the Story-16 run-detail page, its trust boundary, and its evidence browser official platform behavior. Without these spec updates the run-detail page, the 'anyone with project read access sees raw prompts/responses/stderr/tool args' trust boundary, and the evidence whitelist are undocumented.

ROOT CAUSE
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-008-web-ui.md:835-841 has a Run-detail evidence whitelist + run_detail_view stub but no Run-detail-page subsection stating the trust boundary (raw prompts, responses, stderr, tool args visible to project readers) and the evidence whitelist contract (allowed extensions *.txt, *.md, manifest.json, prompt.md, result.json; files < 64KB; no Markdown rendering in the evidence tab).
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:1215-1236 documents a run_detail_view body schema but the Run resolver contract omits prompt, response, stderr, toolCalls(first, after), bundleFiles[], bundleFile(path) with bundle-root confinement, and a normalized ToolCallEntry definition populated at drain time.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-019-agent-evaluation.md:64-83 and :377-383 describe the review surface but do not link it to the /runs/[runId] deep page.

PROPOSED FIX
- FEAT-008: add a Run-detail-page subsection with the trust-boundary statement and the evidence whitelist (allowed extensions + 64KB cap + no Markdown rendering in evidence tab).
- FEAT-010: extend the Run resolver contract with prompt, response, stderr, toolCalls(first, after), bundleFiles[], bundleFile(path) under bundle-root confinement; document ToolCallEntry as the canonical normalized store populated at drain.
- FEAT-019: update the review surface section to link to /runs/[runId].

NON-SCOPE
- Frontend implementation (Story 16.1 / 16.2).
- Server audit-event emission and its test (separate child).
- New auth or RBAC beyond what the trust-boundary statement documents.
- Playwright / e2e changes (separate child).
title: docs: normalize Story 16 run-detail specs across FEAT-008/010/019

acceptance:
1. AGENTS.md contains a Default Interactive Mode section before Bead Policy that routes broad DDx questions to queue_steward and states that execution-ready beads belong to ddx work unless the user explicitly asks for direct implementation.
2. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md defines queue_steward, bead_execution, direct_user_implementation, and review modes, including DDX_MODE names, precedence, allowed actions, prohibited actions, and worker-capacity suggestion rules.
3. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-011-skills.md defines how the ddx skill routes broad interactive prompts versus explicit ddx work/try/bead execution prompts.
4. Add or update TestDDxAgentModeGuidance_DocContract to assert the guidance names all four modes and includes the ddx work carve-out.
5. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run TestDDxAgentModeGuidance_DocContract -count=1 passes.
6. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... passes.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
DDx does not have a written contract that separates broad interactive queue stewardship from worker execution. An agent opened in the repo can interpret queue questions as permission to start edits, while ddx work is meant to pick up execution-ready beads.

ROOT CAUSE
AGENTS.md:1-18 enters bead policy without declaring a default interactive queue-steward mode. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:18-29 defines ddx run/try/work layers but not the interactive mode boundary around them. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-011-skills.md:28-32 routes phrases like "do work", "what's on the queue", and "what's ready" through the ddx skill without specifying whether those prompts mean human intervention triage or worker execution.

PROPOSED FIX
Update AGENTS.md, FEAT-010, and FEAT-011 with an explicit mode taxonomy: queue_steward for broad interactive DDx questions, bead_execution for ddx work/try/execute-bead worker prompts, direct_user_implementation for explicit human requests to edit code/docs, and review for read-only review asks. Define precedence so bead_execution supersedes only the queue-steward default, while tracker, merge-policy, and safety instructions remain load-bearing.

NON-SCOPE
- Implementing CLI behavior beyond static guidance tests.
- Changing tracker status enums or storage.
- Starting or managing workers automatically from interactive sessions.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-09d7d787. Dependencies: none.
title: agent-modes spec: document interactive stewardship modes

description:
PROBLEM
  The execute-bead subsystem files use execute_bead_* naming that conflates the orchestrator, the attempt, the review, and the loop under one prefix, making package boundaries and import graphs ambiguous. The refactor proposal was stored at /tmp/ and is unreadable post-session.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (drain loop), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go (attempt), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go (land coordinator), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go (landing), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go (review), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_triage.go (review triage), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go (disposition), and cli/internal/agent/executions_mirror.go (mirror) all share a flat package with unrelated concerns. The ddx try CLI command named in FEAT-010 does not exist (cli/cmd/try.go absent).

PROPOSED FIX
  Rename per the following table (no behavior change):
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go             -> cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go (already exists; merge or verify)
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go -> cli/internal/agent/try/land.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go         -> cli/internal/agent/try/coordinator.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go       -> cli/internal/agent/try/review.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_triage.go -> cli/internal/agent/try/review_triage.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go       -> cli/internal/agent/try/disposition.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go         -> cli/internal/agent/work/drain.go
    cli/internal/agent/executions_mirror.go         -> cli/internal/agent/try/mirror.go

  Rename identifiers: ExecuteBeadWorker -> work.Worker, ExecuteBeadWorker.Run -> work.Worker.Drain, ExecuteBead -> try.Attempt (FEAT-006 + FEAT-010).

  Add cli/cmd/try.go with cobra command "ddx try <bead-id>" invoking try.Attempt directly (FEAT-010 surface).

  Update FEAT-006 and FEAT-010 spec docs to reflect new package paths. Keep ExecuteBead* shims as typedefs; deletion is C14.

NON-SCOPE
  - Behavior changes to the drain loop or attempt logic.
  - Deletion of ExecuteBead* typedefs (C14).
  - ddx-dc157075 file-path reference migration if that bead has not yet landed.

INTERSECTIONS
- P2: file and identifier names should reflect the subsystem boundary and keep responsibilities separated.
- P5: ddx try as a first-class CLI surface makes the worker/attempt state easier for operators to see.
- P7: the refactor aligns names and prompt surface with the bead-as-prompt contract.

2. `TestReadmeDocumentsPreClaimIntakeContract` asserts the README mentions `readiness_checks`, bool/string/null-or-absent verdict handling, `loop.operator_attention`, same-fingerprint or repeated idle escalation, and claim-success-rate warning knobs.

description:
PROBLEM
Operators lack a concise, durable guide for diagnosing a `ddx work --watch` loop that appears alive but does not claim ready beads. The parent incident's suggested action pointed to route/harness configuration even though the actionable fault was DDx's readiness decoder/worker behavior.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
The user-facing README is the natural top-level diagnostic surface, but the pre-claim intake contract and silent-idle escalation behavior need to be stated there near the quick-start operational commands in `README.md:40-45`. Worker behavior is implemented in the readiness classification and work loop, but operators should not need to read `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` to know what warn-only, operator-attention, and hard errors mean.

PROPOSED FIX
Update the README to name the `readiness_checks` contract, canonical verdict forms, failure-class mapping, same-fingerprint/operator-attention behavior, claim-success-rate warning knobs, and first diagnostic commands/logs for a full ready queue that is idling. Keep the guidance implementation-neutral enough to remain valid as the specific worker internals evolve.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change runtime behavior, decoder behavior, schema validation, Fizeau route/harness configuration, lock handling from ddx-57c40485, or route-resolution wedge handling from ddx-8f2e0ebf. 1. cli/internal/bead/store.go exposes Create(ctx context.Context, b *Bead) error, Update(ctx context.Context, id string, mutate func(*Bead)) error, and Close(ctx context.Context, id string) error with typed parameters and no variadic argument parsing for those methods.
2. cli/internal/bead/backend.go exposes BeadLifecycle.Create(ctx context.Context, b *Bead) error while preserving Apply(ctx context.Context, id string, op Operation) error.
3. All Go callsites for Store.Create, Store.Update, and Store.Close pass an explicit context and typed arguments.
4. Add or update deterministic tests named TestStoreCreate_RejectsCanceledContext, TestStoreUpdate_RejectsCanceledContext, and TestStoreClose_RejectsCanceledContext.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... -run 'TestStore(Create|Update|Close)_RejectsCanceledContext' passes.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... passes.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
TD-027 requires the foundational Store mutation surface to accept context explicitly, but Create, Update, and Close still accept untyped variadic arguments. This hides cancellation behavior and forces the original ddx-464feba2 bead to mix Store API changes with a repo-wide caller migration.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/bead/backend.go:86-89 declares BeadLifecycle with Create(args ...any), while Apply already uses ctx-first shape.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:517-596 exposes Store.Create(args ...any), scans for *Bead, and calls GenID through the variadic path.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:627-655 exposes Store.Update(args ...any), scans for id and mutate function.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1499-1535 exposes Store.Close(args ...any), scans for id and then calls lifecycle helpers without explicit caller context.

PROPOSED FIX
Change only the mutation-side Store methods to typed ctx-first signatures: Create(ctx context.Context, b *Bead), Update(ctx context.Context, id string, mutate func(*Bead)), and Close(ctx context.Context, id string). Update BeadLifecycle in cli/internal/bead/backend.go. Migrate only Create, Update, and Close callsites across the repo to pass context explicitly. Add deterministic bead package tests for canceled-context behavior and typed argument validation.

NON-SCOPE
- Init and GenID signature migration.
- Read APIs: ReadAll, ReadAllFiltered, Get, and GetWithArchive.
- Event method ctx threading: AppendEvent, Events, and EventsByKind.
- Removing workflow helper methods beyond the foundational signatures this bead changes.
- Caller migration unrelated to Create, Update, and Close.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT
- TD-027.

PARENT/DEPS
- Parent/split source: ddx-464feba2.
- Deps: no additional deps; this child can land independently of the setup/id and read signature children.
title: bead store: migrate mutation APIs to ctx-first signatures

2. A genuine uncommitted source/test/doc edit STILL halts the drain (regression test).
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.

description:
PROBLEM
The unattended ddx work drain halts with "worker exited: operator attention required" when a tracked skill SYMLINK is materialized into a real directory by an external skill-install/sync, leaving the project worktree dirty with a non-tracker path. Observed 2026-05-27 06:26: a continuous ddx worker closed 3 beads, then on next pre-claim found .crush/skills/nano-banana-pro-openrouter dirty — git tracks it as a symlink (mode 120000) but the working tree had it as a directory (SKILL.md/assets/scripts) — and exited via the uncommitted-implementation-changes guard. Operator had to restore the symlink (rm + git checkout) and restart the worker. A stray tool-managed skill dir stops the whole queue.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2480 emits "operator attention: project worktree %s has uncommitted implementation changes; released %s" and exits the drain when the landing worktree has any non-allowlisted dirty path (message at execute_bead_loop.go:955-957).
- cli/internal/agent/work/guard.go:38-48 trackerMetadataPaths only allowlists DDx-managed files (added by ddx-df77e668). Tool-managed skill symlinks under .crush/skills/ (and likely .claude/skills, .agents/skills) are not covered, so their symlink->dir materialization reads as implementation dirt and halts the drain.

PROPOSED FIX (implementer/operator picks)
A. Pre-claim: treat a tracked symlink materialized into a dir whose content matches the symlink target as benign (optionally auto-restore the symlink). RECOMMENDED.
B. Pre-claim: allowlist tool-managed skill-dir roots (.crush/skills, .claude/skills, .agents/skills), analogous to trackerMetadataPaths.
C. Repo hygiene: gitignore + untrack these paths (currently committed as symlinks; deliberate structural change).

NON-SCOPE: do not weaken the guard for genuine uncommitted source/test/doc edits; do not change the external skill tooling.
CROSS-REF: extends the tracker-file allowlist from ddx-df77e668.
title: execute-bead: unattended drain halts on tool-managed skill-symlink churn (.crush/skills/*); make pre-claim resilient

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-130cf9ab is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
The decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:

  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --> child_A
  e29892a4 -- parent --> child_B
  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           <- BAD: parent is also a dep
  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  <- BAD

The parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.

Observed scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):
- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')
- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)
- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]
- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores
- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order

Root cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.

There is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Programmatic decomposition path (the action that fans a too-large bead into children) MUST NOT add the parent as a dep. Existing action templates / system prompts are audited and amended; one golden e2e test (decompose_does_not_self_dep) creates a bead with N children and asserts every child's deps set excludes the parent.
- 'ddx bead doctor' (existing subcommand) gains a check 'parent_ancestor_in_deps' that scans the tracker, lists violating beads, and (with --fix) removes the offending edges. Output is stable / scriptable.
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- cd cli && go test ./... passes.
- lefthook run pre-commit passes.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

description:
PROBLEM: The ddx prose checker requires an EXACT Vale version string and neither accepts a compatible range nor installs Vale itself. Operators without precisely that build are hard-blocked, with no bootstrap.
ROOT CAUSE: cli/internal/docprose/vale_runner.go:13 SupportedValeVersion="vale version 3.13.0"; cli/cmd/doctor.go:24 const supportedValeVersion + :631 errors when version != supportedValeVersion (exact-string equality); vale_runner.go:62 exec.LookPath("vale") only — no install/bootstrap. No version-range tolerance and no self-install.
PROPOSED FIX: either (a) accept a documented compatible version range (e.g. >= 3.13.0 within the 3.x line) instead of exact-string equality, or (b) bootstrap the pinned Vale (download the pinned release into a ddx-managed cache dir and invoke that) so the check is self-sufficient on a clean machine. Prefer (b) with (a) as fallback.
NON-SCOPE: changing Vale styles/rules.
title: doctor/prose: Vale check requires exact pinned version and self-installs nothing — make it flexible or bootstrap Vale

acceptance:
1. `TestExecuteBeadLoop_NiflheimEvidence_LandCoordinationFailureDoesNotRerunImplementation` simulates both observed details (`cannot lock ref refs/heads/main` after a candidate/merge is already landed, and `landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s`) and asserts the action is reconcile/retry-land/operator-attention, not a fresh implementation run and not model escalation.
2. The regression shows the worker preserves or reconciles land state instead of rerunning implementation when the land result is already effectively landed or blocked by dirty staged evidence.
3. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'LandCoordination' -count=1` passes.

description:
PROBLEM
Landing failures that are really ref-lock, already-landed, or staged-evidence coordination issues still look like ordinary implementation failures to the worker. That causes fresh implementation reruns and the wrong retry lane instead of reconciliation or operator attention.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:292-312` wraps every `LandingAdvancer` error as a generic `land advancer` failure, which loses whether the candidate was already landed, whether `refs/heads/main` could not be locked, or whether the landing worktree was dirty. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_report_test.go:57-62` only proves the current detail string records land failure; it does not distinguish the coordination class from implementation failure.

PROPOSED FIX
- Split land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures into explicit retry actions: reconcile, retry-land, or operator-attention.
- If the candidate commit or merge is already landed or is an ancestor of the target, reconcile and continue without rerunning implementation.
- If the landing worktree has staged generated evidence or DDx metadata, preserve or retry the land/evidence step according to local policy rather than escalating the model.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change review gating, decomposition detection, or power escalation policy in this child.
- Do not reimplement low-level tracker/index contention fixes owned by sibling work.
title: agent: classify land coordination failures as reconciliation

description:
# PROBLEM
`*Store` exposes 3 dependency-graph methods (`DepAdd`, `DepRemove`, `DepTree`) and 3 queue-ordering methods (`QueueTop`, `QueueMove`, `QueueClear`). Per TD-027 §6 the dep methods belong on `BeadDependencyReader`/`BeadDependencyWriter` sub-interfaces (signature-only change — ctx added). Queue ordering is operator-driven workflow; per TD-027 §14 it belongs in `ops/queue/` helpers with typed `Operation` values.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go` exposes these 6 methods. Foundation declared the `BeadDependencyReader`/`BeadDependencyWriter` interfaces but ctx isn't yet on `*Store`'s dep methods. `BeadQueue` isn't an interface in TD-027 — queue ops are workflow concerns that live as helpers.

# PROPOSED FIX

## Dependencies (signature-only)

1. Update `*Store.DepAdd(ctx, id, depID)`, `DepRemove(ctx, id, depID)`, `DepTree(ctx, rootID)` to take ctx.
2. Compile-time assertions: `var _ BeadDependencyReader = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadDependencyWriter = (*Store)(nil)`.
3. Thread ctx through callers. Narrow field types opportunistically (e.g., `ddx bead dep tree` CLI handler can narrow to `BeadDependencyReader`).

## Queue (helper-migration; methods removed)

4. Add named `Operation` types to `cli/internal/bead/operation.go`:
   - `QueueSetTop{}` — move bead to top of queue (sparse rank ordering).
   - `QueueSetPosition{Position int}` — operator move-to-position.
   - `QueueClearOp{}` — remove queue rank.
5. Create `cli/internal/bead/ops/queue/queue.go`:
   - `Top(ctx, l, id) error` — wraps `QueueSetTop{}`.
   - `Move(ctx, l, id, position int) error` — wraps `QueueSetPosition`.
   - `Clear(ctx, l, id) error` — wraps `QueueClearOp{}`.
6. Migrate callers (operator queue manipulation in `cli/cmd/bead.go` queue subcommands) from `*Store.QueueTop`/`QueueMove`/`QueueClear` → `ops/queue/` helpers.
7. Remove `*Store.QueueTop`, `QueueMove`, `QueueClear`.

# NON-SCOPE
- Dependency cycle detection — handled at higher layer.
- Queue rank renormalization algorithm — internal to ops/queue; sparse-rank logic remains as-is.
- Other concerns — separate beads.
- Module boundary lockdown — `ddx-74452926`.

# Acceptance criteria

1. `*Store.DepAdd`, `DepRemove`, `DepTree` take ctx. Compile-time assertions for `BeadDependencyReader` and `BeadDependencyWriter` compile.
2. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` declares `QueueSetTop`, `QueueSetPosition{Position}`, `QueueClearOp` types with `Apply(b *Bead) error` impls. Tests: `TestOperation_QueueSetTop_AssignsTopRank`, `TestOperation_QueueSetPosition_RespectsBucket`, `TestOperation_QueueClearOp_RemovesRank`.
3. `cli/internal/bead/ops/queue/` package exists with helpers + tests: `TestOpsQueue_Top_MovesBeadToFront`, `TestOpsQueue_Move_PreservesPriorityBucket`, `TestOpsQueue_Clear_RemovesOperatorOverride`.
4. `*Store` no longer exposes `QueueTop`, `QueueMove`, `QueueClear`. Verified by grep.
5. Caller files no longer reference these methods on `*Store` outside the bead package.
6. Queue-ordering invariants (SD-004 §"Ready queue" / queue derivation) preserved — operator overrides apply within a priority bucket; explicit rank wins over missing rank within bucket. Test: `TestQueueOrdering_OperatorRankWithinBucket`.
7. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

# Labels
phase:iterate, area:bead, area:workflow, area:dep, kind:refactor, spec:TD-027

# Parent + Deps
Parent: ddx-8d747049
Deps: ddx-c6317784.

description:
PROBLEM
Interactive queue stewardship needs a higher-level surface than ddx bead ready. Ready beads are worker-actionable and should usually be left to ddx work; operators need a command that exposes work ddx ready/work will not pick up plus a conservative signal when the ready queue is deep enough to justify starting another worker.

ROOT CAUSE
.agents/skills/ddx/reference/status.md:7-16 lists ready, blocked, status, and list as queue inspection commands but has no human-intervention view. .agents/skills/ddx/reference/status.md:18-23 answers "What should I work on next?" with ddx bead ready. cli/cmd/work_plan.go:119-160 prints execution plan rows and skipped epics, but not a first-class intervention queue or worker-capacity recommendation.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a read-only command surface, proposed as ddx work focus, that summarizes human-required intervention separately from worker-ready work. It should show needs_human/review-blocked beads, blocked beads that require planning or dependency decisions, malformed or underspecified beads if lifecycle signals are available, ready-depth summary, active-worker/in-progress signals when observable, and unknown capacity hazards when not observable. It may recommend starting another worker only from observable queue depth/capacity signals.

NON-SCOPE
- Automatically starting workers.
- Closing, updating, or splitting beads.
- Replacing ddx work plan or ddx bead ready.

description:
PROBLEM
When readiness honors a prior operator promotion and skips a downgrade, the intake.blocked entry it writes is indistinguishable from any other warning. Operators cannot tell that readiness deliberately deferred to a prior acceptance.

ROOT CAUSE

PROPOSED FIX
Extend the intake.blocked entry schema with an operator_override boolean. Plumb the sibling-guard's operator-promotion determination into the entry construction site so operator_override=true is written when readiness skipped a downgrade because the bead was operator-promoted, and false otherwise. Consume the operator-promotion detection function provided by the sibling implementation bead.

description:
PROBLEM
Slow attempt workspace preparation can monopolize `.ddx/.git-tracker.lock` and serialize otherwise parallel workers. `execute_bead` currently holds `withTrackerLock` across tracker commit, dirty checkpoint, base resolution, and `attemptBackend.Prepare`. The worktree backend performs `git worktree add`; local-clone/docker-clone can perform clone, checkout, and runtime setup, which are filesystem-heavy under large repos or disk pressure.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1499-1534 includes `attemptBackend.Prepare` inside the `withTrackerLock` critical section.
- cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend.go:101-123 runs `git worktree add` for the worktree backend.
- cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend.go:150-184 runs local clone and checkout for the local-clone backend.
- cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend.go:211-235 layers docker runtime setup on top of local clone.

PROPOSED FIX
- Narrow the main-git lock to the parent-repo mutations that require serialization: tracker commit, dirty checkpoint, and base resolution.
- Move slow clone/docker setup outside the lock, or split attempt preparation into a brief locked registration step plus unlocked filesystem setup.
- Add channel-blocking fake backend coverage so the test fails if slow preparation holds the tracker lock.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not remove linked-worktree/local-clone/docker-clone attempt backends.
- Do not introduce network or external agent dependencies into tests.
title: agent: move slow attempt preparation outside the main-git lock

description:
PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

There is no first-time machine setup command, and `ddx init` bundles plugin install with manifest creation, forcing a plugin write into every newly initialized project instead of lazy-resolving from the global tier.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/init.go bundles plugin install with manifest creation; there is no `--global` umbrella path that installs the default `ddx` plugin globally and writes global config.

PROPOSED FIX
1. `ddx init --global` is the umbrella first-time-setup command: installs the default `ddx` plugin globally to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx (via GlobalPluginDir), creates agent-tier links (~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx pointing into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/), and writes ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults.
2. `ddx init` (no flag) creates <project>/.ddx/ and its manifest but does NOT install plugins; plugins are lazy-resolved on first use, preferring the project tier over global per the registry precedence bead.

GOVERNING ARTIFACTS
- Epic ddx-602ae31a, Principle 2.

description:
PROBLEM
The public DDx execution vocabulary is supposed to be `ddx run`, `ddx try`, and `ddx work`, but large parts of the codebase still use legacy `execute-bead` / `work` names in public help, comments, server worker kinds, GraphQL models, tests, docs, tracker metadata helpers, and internal symbols. Operators now see mixed terms and developers keep adding new code under the old names.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1485-1563 still defines the legacy `ddx agent work` cobra surface as if it were primary: help says "work is the primary queue-driven execution surface", examples use `ddx agent work`, and deprecation text still names work rather than pointing to `ddx work`.
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1590-1940 keeps the main work implementation named `runAgentExecuteLoopImpl`, `writeExecuteLoopResult`, and `ExecuteBeadLoopResult`, so new work-path changes naturally copy the old terminology.
- cli/cmd/try.go:213-333 implements the new `ddx try` command by constructing `ExecuteBeadExecutor`, `ExecuteBeadRuntime`, `ExecuteBeadWorker`, and `ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime`, so the new public command still routes through legacy names.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/models.go:2233-2281 and cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/workers.spec.ts:343-397 expose worker kind/comment strings as `work` / `execute-bead`, which leaks legacy naming through API and UI fixtures.
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:215-262 still describes attempt results and retry metadata as `execute-bead` / `work-*`, even while FEAT-010 and ADR-023 name the lifecycle `try` / `work`.
- Existing related beads are partially stale or narrower than this repo-wide cleanup: ddx-387a0178 owns C13 file/identifier renames and still says `ddx try` does not exist, while ddx-29058e2a / ddx-1a9cc01f still propose `ExecuteLoopSpec` naming for a work-side spec.

WHY THE NAMES DIVERGED
The codebase grew from the historical `ddx agent execute-bead` and `ddx agent work` commands. FEAT-010 introduced the clearer layered vocabulary (`run` = invocation atom, `try` = one bead attempt, `work` = queue drain), and `ddx try` / `ddx work` now exist as the intended operator surface. The implementation retained old symbols and compatibility aliases, so old names persisted as the easiest local pattern to copy.

PROPOSED FIX
- Perform a repo-wide terminology audit using `rg -n "work|ExecuteLoop|execute loop|execute-bead|ExecuteBead|execute bead"` and classify each hit as: public/user-facing rename, internal compatibility alias to retain, durable metadata key to retain with documentation, historical docs intentionally retained, or generated fixture/test data.
- Update public/user-facing CLI help, comments, API/GraphQL descriptions, server/frontend labels, tests, and docs to prefer `work` and `try`.
- Rename non-compatibility internal types/functions introduced by the FEAT-010 path from ExecuteLoop/ExecuteBead vocabulary to Work/Try vocabulary. Coordinate with ddx-387a0178 C13; if C13 already performs a rename, fold this cleanup into or after it rather than duplicating work.
- For compatibility aliases and durable data keys that must remain (`ddx agent work`, `ddx agent execute-bead`, `work-*` Extra keys, historical event kinds), add concise comments documenting them as legacy/back-compat names and point readers at `ddx work` / `ddx try`.
- Re-scope or update related open beads whose titles/descriptions still establish new `ExecuteLoop*` names (notably ddx-29058e2a and ddx-1a9cc01f) so future work does not add fresh legacy terminology.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not remove the legacy `ddx agent work` or `ddx agent execute-bead` command aliases in this bead; they are compatibility surfaces unless a governing doc explicitly authorizes removal.
- Do not rename durable tracker keys or historical event kinds if doing so would break existing `.ddx/beads.jsonl`, `.ddx/executions`, worker records, or archive reads. Document retained keys instead.
- Do not change work/try behavior, queue ordering, claim semantics, routing policy, or Fizeau integration.
- Do not hand-edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl`; update any related bead metadata only with `ddx bead update` / `ddx bead dep`.

INTERSECTIONS
- ddx-387a0178: structural C13 file/identifier rename; this bead depends on it or should be folded into it if it is re-authored.
- ddx-29058e2a and ddx-1a9cc01f: ExecuteLoopSpec naming conflicts with the intended WorkSpec/WorkRequest vocabulary; update or supersede as part of this cleanup.
- ddx-eccc6efb: server-spawn migration may delete some `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` paths; if it lands first, shrink this bead to remaining public/API/doc names.

title: agent: replace legacy execute terminology with work/try nomenclature

description:
PROBLEM
Even once intake.blocked entries carry rule_fingerprint and operator_override, operators cannot see those fields because `ddx bead show` does not render them in the intake.blocked list section.

ROOT CAUSE
- The intake.blocked rendering path in `ddx bead show` (cli/cmd/bead_show.go and its formatter helpers) prints only outcome and detail strings per entry; it does not read or print rule_fingerprint or operator_override.

PROPOSED FIX
Update the `ddx bead show` formatter for intake.blocked entries to read and render rule_fingerprint and operator_override per entry. Keep the rendering compact and stable so existing snapshot/golden tests can be updated deterministically.

NON-SCOPE
- Defining or computing rule_fingerprint (sibling child bead).
- Defining or propagating operator_override (sibling child bead).
- Adding new readiness rules or changing rule evaluation logic.
- Changing the readiness terminal flow itself.
- Updating FEAT-010/ADR-023 policy text.
- Fizeau concrete route selection, model ranking, provider fallback, or profile catalog behavior.
- Rewriting execute-bead branch merge policy.
- Changing post-attempt review terminal outcomes that already carry concrete evidence for operator action.

PARENT/DEPS
Parent: ddx-cc0641a7. Depends on both sibling child beads (fingerprint+dedup, operator_override) so the fields exist on entries to render.

acceptance:
1. TestBeadAutoCommitSerializesThroughMainGitLock (or equivalent) runs two or more concurrent bead update/create operations with auto_commit=always against the same repo and proves all complete without .git/index.lock errors.
2. The same regression asserts commit history is audit-correct: each auto-commit either contains only the operation named in its subject or is an explicitly named combined commit that lists every included tracker mutation.
3. Existing stale-lock recovery tests TestBeadCreateAutoCommitRecoversStaleIndexLock, TestBeadUpdateAutoCommitRecoversStaleIndexLock, and TestBeadCloseAutoCommitRecoversStaleIndexLock remain green.
4. cd cli && go test ./cmd -run 'TestBead(AutoCommitSerializesThroughMainGitLock|CreateAutoCommitRecoversStaleIndexLock|UpdateAutoCommitRecoversStaleIndexLock|CloseAutoCommitRecoversStaleIndexLock)' -count=1 passes.
5. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/bead/... ./internal/gitlock/... ./internal/agent/... passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
During the current monitor on 2026-05-15, a worker executing ddx-bdc0065e cancelled several stale beads. Multiple ddx bead update commands ran close together from the execute-bead context. The tracker ended clean, but the auto-commit audit trail was noisy: one commit subject said it updated ddx-387a0178 while it also picked up additional bead-row changes, another update failed with .git/index.lock, and another commit reported nothing to commit. That makes tracker history misleading and is the same class of dirty/index-lock noise the operator wants eliminated.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/cmd/bead.go:134-154 builds bead auto-commit config and calls bead.AutoCommitFilesWithRecovery directly. cli/internal/bead/git_autocommit.go:10-17 wraps git add/commit with gitlock.RunGitWithIndexLockRecovery, but that only recovers from stale/transient .git/index.lock; it does not serialize the whole add/commit pair under the process-shared main git lock. cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go:98-118 documents and implements withMainGitLock/withTrackerLock for primary-checkout git writes, and cli/internal/gitlock/gitlock.go:22-24 says DDx writers serialize through that lock, but the bead CLI auto-commit path is outside it.

PROPOSED FIX
- Route bead CLI auto-commit add/commit operations through the same process-shared main git lock used by landing and pre-dispatch checkpointing.
- Preserve existing stale index.lock recovery inside the lock, so stale locks are still repaired and live owners still fail or wait according to policy.
- Add a concurrent regression where multiple bead update/create auto-commits run against one repo and produce either deterministic serialized commits or one explicitly combined commit; no index.lock failures, no misleading commit subjects that omit paths actually included.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change .ddx/beads.jsonl format.
- Do not remove auto-commit recovery for stale index locks.
- Do not squash, amend, or rewrite existing execute-bead history.
- Do not hand-edit .ddx/beads.jsonl.

DEPS
None.
title: bead: serialize auto-commit tracker git writes

description:
PROBLEM
The foundation bead for TD-027 split the bead storage migration into caller-facing and package-local slices, but the current `ddx-464feba2` child still bundles the Store API migration together with a repo-wide caller sweep. The local `cli/internal/bead` package work is still a valid, self-contained slice: `cli/internal/bead/backend.go` already declares ctx-first interfaces, while `cli/internal/bead/store.go` still exposes the legacy zero-arg methods (`Init`, `ReadAll`, `Get`, `Create`, `Update`, `Close`, `GenID`).

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:74-100` declares the ctx-first foundation interfaces.
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go:140-1181` still exposes the legacy no-ctx Store methods.
- `ddx-464feba2` was blocked because the broader caller migration exceeds that child slice.

PROPOSED FIX
Update only the core bead package surface so the Store methods in `cli/internal/bead/store.go` accept `ctx context.Context` as first parameter for the foundational non-event methods, and keep the verification local to `cli/internal/bead/...`. Do not touch the external caller sweep in this bead. Update or add the store/package tests needed to prove the local API still behaves correctly after the signature change.

NON-SCOPE
- Caller migration in `cli/cmd`, `cli/internal/agent`, `cli/internal/server`, `cli/internal/escalation`, and metrics packages.
- Event method ctx threading (`AppendEvent`, `Events`, `EventsByKind`).
- Removing workflow helper methods beyond the foundational signatures this bead changes.

ACCEPTANCE
1. The foundational Store methods in `cli/internal/bead/store.go` take `ctx context.Context` first: `Init`, `ReadAll`, `ReadAllFiltered`, `Get`, `GetWithArchive`, `Create`, `Update`, `Close`, and `GenID`.
2. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/...` passes.
3. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
4. Any tests added or updated in `cli/internal/bead/` are named and deterministic, with no caller-package changes in this bead.

Current state of the foundational Store methods (cli/internal/bead/store.go): Init(args ...any) error                                  // line 149 GenID(args ...any) (string, error)                       // line 167 ReadAll(args ...any) ([]Bead, error)                     // line 182 ReadAllFiltered(args ...any) ([]Bead, error)             // line 224 Create(args ...any) error                                // line 518 Get(args ...any) (*Bead, error)                          // line 598 Update(args ...any) error                                // line 627 Close(args ...any) error                                 // line 1426 And in archive.go: GetWithArchive(id string) (*Bead, error)                 // line 172 Each variadic method extracts ctx via storeCallContext(args) and accepts either `s.Init()` or `s.Init(context.Background())` today. 2. AC1 requires these methods to take `ctx context.Context` as a typed first parameter (matching the BeadEventReader / BeadQueries shape already in cli/internal/bead/backend.go:91-114). Any concrete signature satisfying AC1 — `Init(ctx context.Context) error`, `Init(ctx context.Context, args ...any) error`, etc. — rejects the current zero-arg `s.Init()` callsites that exist in external packages. 3. The bead's PROPOSED FIX and NON-SCOPE explicitly forbid touching external callers ("Do not touch the external caller sweep in this bead"; "NON-SCOPE: Caller migration in cli/cmd, cli/internal/agent, cli/internal/server, cli/internal/escalation, and metrics packages.") and AC4 requires "no caller-package changes in this bead." 4. AC3 requires `lefthook run pre-commit` to pass, which runs `go build -buildvcs=false -v ./...` from cli/ (lefthook.yml:122-129). That build covers every package in the module, including all the external caller packages NON-SCOPE forbids touching. 5. Concrete external breakage if AC1 is implemented today (`grep -rE '\b(store|s)\.(Init|Create|Update|Get|Close|ReadAll|GenID|GetWithArchive|ReadAllFiltered)\(' cli/ --include='*.go'`): - 174 Go files reference these Store methods. - 73 distinct files outside cli/internal/bead/ use the zero-arg `store.Init()` form (e.g. cli/cmd/ac_test.go:37, cli/cmd/bead_human_test.go:20, cli/internal/agent/..., cli/internal/server/...). - Production-code stragglers without ctx exist too: cli/cmd/bead.go:414  s.GetWithArchive(args[0]) cli/cmd/bead.go:1281 s.Close(id) cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4485 store.Get(id) cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4811 store.Update(beadID, ...) `go build ./...` and the lefthook go-build hook will fail at every one of those callsites the instant Init/Get/Update/Close/ReadAll/GenID/ GetWithArchive/ReadAllFiltered become non-variadic ctx-first. There is no signature shape that satisfies AC1 while preserving all current call patterns (Go does not allow same-name method overloads on a single receiver type). 6. Step 0 size envelope is also exceeded for the only resolution that preserves AC1+AC3: the necessary mechanical caller sweep crosses ~170 files in five packages (cmd, internal/agent, internal/server, internal/attemptmetrics, internal/agentmetrics) — well past the "~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages" guardrail. That is exactly the sweep that NON-SCOPE excludes, and the parent split (TD-027 / ddx-464feba2) was rejected for the same reason. Operator action requested: Re-scope this bead to one of: (a) Add the caller-package migration to scope (delete the NON-SCOPE block and AC4's "no caller-package changes"). This becomes the original TD-027 sweep that previously failed as too large; if pursued, expect a multi-file diff across cli/cmd, cli/internal/agent, cli/internal/server, cli/internal/attemptmetrics, cli/internal/agentmetrics. (b) Replace AC3 with a narrower verification (e.g. "go test ./internal/bead/... and go vet ./internal/bead/... pass"), and explicitly accept that `go build ./...` and lefthook pre-commit will be red until follow-up caller-migration beads land. The follow-up beads would then re-establish AC3 once the sweep is complete. (c) Replace AC1 with an additive shape that does not rename or retype the existing variadic methods — e.g. "add new ctx-first Store methods with distinct names (InitCtx / GetCtx / …) so the legacy variadic surface stays in place during the migration", then a follow-up bead deletes the variadic methods after the caller sweep finishes. This satisfies the spirit of TD-027 (introducing the typed ctx-first surface in the bead package first) without breaking the build outside the package. No code changes were made on this attempt.
